hypotheses.label: naive's blocking-side corpus actually drives the deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild call chain to the null-parent gate true-side per seed, while naive_ngram4's corpus stays mired in shallow tokenizer states and never reaches it

params.note: winner_fz/loser_fz map to resolving_seeds(naive)/blocking_seeds(naive_ngram4) in db; libxml2 cov image = docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov (owning server = sB)

hypotheses.prediction: G1: when each arm's saved seeds are replayed through the libxml2 cov binary, naive's resolving seeds take the BLOCKED true-side of tree.c:3421 (the analysis observed L3421 T=18) at a far higher per-seed rate than naive_ngram4's blocking seeds (analysis: ngram4 L3421 true=0, false=194) => side_lift >> 1 AND winner_deeper == true. The discriminated ALTERNATIVE: if the divergence were mere stochastic energy noise rather than a structural deep-path-reach gap, both arms' corpora would reach the gate true-side comparably (side_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper == false). The measurement can come out either way, so it is falsifiable AND it separates a genuine path-reach mechanism from a non-path artifact. G2-independent: depth_reach measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches at the gate line, NOT the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: ngram-4 feedback floods the corpus with near-duplicate shallow-tokenizer seeds, starving energy from the rare deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild path that naive reaches (PRIOR; corpus-SCALE energy story)
hypotheses.prediction: G1: naive_ngram4 corpus is inflated with shallow-gram entries (corpus_size_ratio >> 1) while naive reaches deeper. A non-saturation mechanism predicts comparable corpus sizes.
hypotheses.non_decidable_reason: REFUTED-as-non-discriminable in round 0 and NOT re-tested: corpus_size_ratio measures a whole-campaign corpus-SCALE / energy-allocation property that cannot be attributed to this single branch (it cannot separate ngram saturation-starvation from plain non-mutation of the '&...;' bytes for ONE branch, and n=1 gives no cross-branch threshold support). The per-branch deep-path-reach question this prior was really asking is captured instead by the new ngram_deep_path_reach_gap hypothesis (depth_reach), tried FIRST. Kept here only as the documented refuted/superseded record.

data_realities_respected: Cross-server (rule #4): libxml2 is owned by server B (sB corpora = lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); it is NOT marked decidable:false merely because libxml2 is absent from the s4 on-disk set (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3). The depth_reach test is designed to run on the OWNING server, which has libxml2 corpora + the cov image (docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov).

depth_reach is a seed_set Docker tool (libafl-libxml2-cov image) — needs resolving_seeds(naive) + blocking_seeds(naive_ngram4) in db/blockers.sqlite for branch 6939; the rep set carries these. Per-seed normalization handles unequal arm sizes (the corpus-size confound that sank the prior corpus_size_ratio test).

no seed_lineage 'ngram event' proposed — ngram-4 is a coverage FEEDBACK, untagged in mutation_op (analogous to the value_profile feedback invisibility); its effect is measured only via what each arm's corpus REACHES, never via a lineage event.
no byte_freq_ratio proposed — the gate is presence/non-null structural (parent==NULL), not a fixed-offset value gate; byte-alignment is inapplicable.
corpus_size_ratio (the prior) is the genuinely non-attributable whole-campaign scale property; it stays decidable:false and is superseded, not relaxed.
notes: target=libxml2 (owning server = sB). PIVOT from round 0: the round-0 verdict marked this branch decidable:false on the strength of 'libxml2 has no on-disk corpus' — but that was the s4 corpus list; libxml2 IS available on its owning server, so cross-server rule #4 forbids pre-deciding it false. Re-examined the BUILT tools: depth_reach is the right discriminator. The branch's own analysis already states the path-reach divergence in depth_reach's exact terms — naive's seeds take the blocked true-side at tree.c:3421 (L3421 T=18, return NULL at 3426-3427) while naive_ngram4's seeds sit at true=0/false=194, an order of magnitude deeper into shallow scanners. depth_reach (seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch, ITERATION/path-DEPTH subtype, anchor harfbuzz/6585, validated 7/20) replays both arms' corpora through the cov binary and reports per-seed blocked-side reach: side_lift >> 1 + winner_deeper confirms a genuine deep-path-reach mechanism and rules out stochastic-noise. KEPT the prior corpus_size_ratio hypothesis as decidable:false (superseded, NOT relaxed) because corpus-scale energy starvation is a whole-campaign property non-attributable to one branch. NEW mechanism = ngram_deep_path_reach_gap, tried first. If the owning server lacks the naive_ngram4 blocking seeds in the DB or the depth replay shows side_lift~1, the branch honestly routes to fallback inconclusive (G3).
